templates.name: Deckbuilder (Slay-the-Spire-like)
templates.description: Roguelike deckbuilder (Slay-the-Spire-like) on chun92/card-framework. The COMBAT is JSON-defined cards (Strike/Cleave/Defend/Insight/Surge, generated blockout art) + a full draw/play/discard loop (5-card hand refill, 3 energy/turn, drag-to-play resolving damage/block/draw/energy, discard reshuffles when dry, End Turn discards the hand and the enemy attacks through your block). Wrapping it is a full ROGUELIKE RUN engine in GameManager (pure, seedable, headless-testable): the game opens on a MAP hub (scenes/map.tscn) — a generated, connected floor-by-floor node DAG (combat / elite / event / rest / boss) you route through; your HP, gold, deck and relics PERSIST across fights; wins earn gold + a card reward (pick 1 of 3), elites/boss grant relics (bonus energy / start-block / heal-on-kill, queried by combat), rest nodes heal, the boss ends the run. Combat reads the run when one is active (persisted HP, grown deck, the node's scaled encounter, relic mods) and reports the result back; with no active run the combat scene still plays standalone against the default Cog-Golem, so the base loop + its boot probe are unchanged. Kit decision (2026-07-11): DesirePathGames/Slay-The-Robot was evaluated per the roadmap but it is a complete game (no addon payload) — kept as an MIT reference; card-framework is the vendorable engine (containers, drag-drop, JSON factory; Godot 4.6 pin).

templates.description: Top-down action-adventure base (classic Zelda structure), pure first-party (per the Wave-3 survey no clearly-maintained MIT Zelda kit exists, and the adventure layer is build-cheap on top of the top-down-action archetype): a 4-directional hero with a sword-arc sweep (distance+angle hit-test against the enemies group), an item button driving the equipped-item slot (ships the boomerang — out-and-return flight that stuns enemies), and a 3-room world on a screen grid with Zelda-style room transitions: the camera snaps to whichever room the player walks into and each room wakes only its own enemies; rooms build their floor/perimeter walls (with doorway gaps) in code so scenes stay lean. Dungeon fundamentals: a small-key pickup, a locked door that consumes the key on bump, a latching pressure-plate puzzle opening a switch door, and a treasure chest granting + equipping the boomerang via the interact button — every key/door/plate/chest writes a GameManager flag so world state survives room transitions and scene reloads. Two enemy archetypes on a shared chassis (ping-pong patroller, straight-line chaser) with touch damage on cooldown, sword take_hit, boomerang stun, and chance heart drops; hearts row + key count + equipped-item HUD. Standard NoxDev ABI (Master/Music/SFX buses, player/game_manager/persistent groups, save_data() contracts — player saves hearts/keys/items, GameManager saves flags — scalable_text, pause action).

templates.name: Crowd Rush (hypercasual crowd-runner)
templates.description: Hypercasual 3D crowd-runner base (Count-Masters-like), pure first-party (per the Wave-3 survey no maintained MIT crowd-runner kit exists and the genre is build-cheap: one MultiMesh, disc-formation math, count arithmetic): an auto-forward crowd of capsule units running a straight track with horizontal steering (A/D-arrows or mouse-x), every unit drawn by ONE MultiMesh in a phyllotaxis disc around the leader — no per-unit nodes, no physics bodies, 400 slots allocated so 200+ units stay cheap. Multiplier/add gates in choice pairs ("+10"/"-5", "-5"/"x2") consumed by the leader's lane on crossing; a spike-strip obstacle that kills the individual units sweeping through its box; a blocking enemy crowd resolved by 1:1 annihilation at a fixed rate (the bigger crowd survives with exactly the difference, a tie wipes the run); and a finish line + end-tower comparison (strictly more survivors than the tower count = win, score = surviving units). Crowd-count/distance HUD plus floating 3D count labels over both crowds; finish or wipe opens a run summary (survivors/tower/distance, best-survivors + best-distance flags on GameManager, Enter restarts). Standard NoxDev ABI (Master/Music/SFX buses, player/game_manager/persistent groups, save_data() contracts, scalable_text, pause action).

templates.name: Dungeon Crawler Classic (grid-based first-person)
templates.description: Grid-based first-person dungeon crawler base (Lands of Lore: The Throne of Chaos / Eye of the Beholder structure), pure first-party (per the Wave-3 survey no maintained MIT grid-crawler kit exists and the genre is build-cheap: the whole illusion is grid math + tweens): the camera IS the party — a Node3D stepped cell-by-cell (forward/back/strafe, 90-degree turns, each smoothed with a short tween) through a dungeon built entirely in code from one ASCII map constant (14x13 starter: rooms, corridors, a plain bump-open door, a locked door + key, a lever-opened secret wall, torch-lit fog). NO physics anywhere — movement, door bumps, lever pulls, pickups, combat reach and enemy pathing are all grid queries, which keeps everything headless-robust and deterministic (zero RNG). Party of 3 (two front-row melee + a back-row caster whose 3-cell spark bolt costs MP): per-member HP/MP, per-member attack buttons with cooldowns (portrait click or hotkeys 1-3), front row soaks enemy melee, back row exposed only when both fronts fall. Real-time enemies occupy cells, step toward the party on move timers (greedy axis chase respecting doors/occupancy) and melee when cardinally adjacent. Pickups on walk-over (key; potions drunk via button heal the lowest-HP member); doors/lever/pickups write GameManager flags and self-restore so the dungeon stays solved across reloads. Classic bottom-bar HUD: scrolling message log, 3 portrait panels (ColorRect placeholder + HP/MP bars — Studio-bound art slots for the lol-vga style pack), compass from facing, keys/potions/kills. Party wipe opens a run summary (kills, secret found, best-kills flag, Enter restarts). Standard NoxDev ABI (Master/Music/SFX buses, player/game_manager/persistent groups, save_data() contracts, scalable_text, pause action).

templates.name: Gamebook IF (playable computed if-engine gamebook)
templates.description: The PLAYABLE standard single-player gamebook on the computed Nox Loom if-engine (nox_if_engine, spec P0-P2) -- a data-driven text-RPG that plays 100% WITHOUT any AI or networking. The nox_if_engine addon is VENDORED into addons/ (self-contained: the seedable dice roller, the generic data-driven resolver, the narrative-graph scenario model, dual-tier characters, one-off + campaign runners, and save/load), and this template is the playable UI that DRIVES it. A PlaySession autoload owns the active runner (IFOneOffRunner or IFCampaignRunner) and is the single bridge the UI talks to (the if-engine analogue of ff-gamebook's SessionState). The play scene renders the current passage (illustrated plate + title + text), builds CHOICE BUTTONS gated by the engine's conditions (effects applied on choose), surfaces the resolver's rolls in a dice tray (roll-under / meet-or-beat / threshold-bands, ruleset-agnostic), and shows a live adventure sheet + item.* inventory HUD built from the chosen ruleset's sheetTemplate. Supports BOTH a one-off adventure (Thornwood Crypt, reusing the engine's thornwood-crypt scenario + ff-2d6 ruleset) AND a linked multi-module campaign (Crown of Embers) with carried world state, dual-tier characters, and save/resume between modules via IFSaveGame. AI is an OPTIONAL, DISABLED-BY-DEFAULT, documented-inert seam (the AiDm autoload -- the place the future P4 AI-DM layer plugs in): there is NO LLM and NO fake AI; every call site is guarded by 'if AiDm.enabled' so the computed game is byte-identical with it off. Standard NoxDev ABI (Master/Music/SFX buses, game_manager/persistent groups, save_data() contracts, scalable_text, pause action) plus the asset-binding manifest (assets.manifest.json + AssetBinder) shared with ff-gamebook for illustrated plates. Does NOT modify the if-engine addon -- it consumes it.

Shipped IN the skeleton (not cloned at scaffold) so the template is self-contained. Consume-only: keep it in sync with skills/if-engine's copy; do not edit it here.

templates.name: Bullet-Hell (Unity)
templates.description: Unity port of the bullet-hell archetype (danmaku / shmup), pure first-party. Godot's bullet-hell is built on the third-party BulletUpHell addon (bullets as pooled shapes in one shared Area2D); the Unity lane forbids third-party kits, so the pooled-bullet engine is reimplemented first-party in C#: BulletSystem is an auto-creating singleton with an object pool, id-keyed BulletProps/SpawnPattern registries, a registered Player special target, a BulletHitBody event, and one central FixedUpdate that steps every live bullet (movement, lifetime, out-of-box death, collision). A standard bullet + a 12-way ring pattern fire from a Spawner every 0.8s; the player ship has 8-directional kinematic movement, a held-focus slowdown, a small 0.12u hurtbox, and 3 lives with grace + respawn. Code-built demo scene via the NoxBootstrap editor script (no committed .unity files); GameManager singleton with world flags and JSON save/load mirroring the NoxDev ABI save_data() contract via ISaveable (shared with top-down-action-unity).

templates.name: Euro Engine-Builder (competitive resource→production→VP engine, 2D)
templates.description: Competitive Euro-style ENGINE-BUILDER board game (Scythe/Wingspan/Wyrmspan lineage): every player grows a resource→production→victory-point engine and the best engine wins. The WHOLE rules engine is a pure, seedable, headless-testable RefCounted class (EuroEngine) owned by a GameManager autoload — 1 human + up to 4 heuristic-AI opponents (2–5 total). 5 tracked resources (wood/grain/metal/coin/energy) with a strict conservation ledger (pool == start + produced − spent, proven every turn); a 25-card development deck (5 categories, 3 copies each) with per-card cost/output/VP shuffled by a seeded RNG; a shared action board of 5 action types (PRODUCE/BUILD/TRADE/RESEARCH/DEPLOY) where a turn is exactly ONE legal action (is_legal rejects out-of-turn/unaffordable/malformed); 3 objective-token types (build 3 mining cards / reach 3 stars / bank 12 coin) claimed first-come, plus end-game majorities (most stars/cards/coin), plus VP from built cards and goal-stars; an end trigger (first to 6 goal-stars via DEPLOY, else 18 rounds) → final_scoring with a single deterministic winner. The AI is a genuine NON-LLM heuristic: it enumerates every legal action and scores each by a weighted evaluation (immediate VP + resource-efficiency + engine growth + quadratic progress-to-goal + option/conversion value), deterministic index tie-break, never illegal, never stalls. Full to_dict/from_dict save-load (banks, tableaus, hands, stars, deck, objectives, cursor, RNG state). board.gd renders the action board + per-player panels (resources/VP/stars/production/tableau) + objectives + current-player indicator + turn log, enabling only legal actions for the active human seat and auto-resolving AI turns. PLAY MODES — a SEAT-CONTROLLER matrix (STAGE 1): every one of the 2–5 seats carries a ControllerKind — HUMAN_LOCAL (the turn dispatcher blocks for local UI input), AI_HEURISTIC (auto-resolves via ai_choose), or the OPTIONAL AI_LLM (a local-LLM-assisted seat) — and GameManager is the turn dispatcher that asks whose-turn/what-kind. Supported modes: all-AI, single-player 1-human-vs-heuristic-AI (the default preset, unchanged), and LOCAL HOTSEAT pass-and-play (2+ HUMAN_LOCAL seats on one machine) with a 'pass the device' hand-off banner shown before every human turn after the first, plus an OPTIONAL LLM-ASSIST seat (AI_LLM): a local LLM (default Ollama http://localhost:11434, host/model in [euro_llm] project settings) is shown the seat's state + the ENUMERATED legal actions and picks one, which is re-validated via is_legal/apply_action — fully OFFLINE-SAFE, falling back to the deterministic ai_choose heuristic on ANY failure (provider disabled, unreachable, timeout, non-2xx, unparseable, or an illegal/out-of-range pick), so an AI_LLM seat with no provider plays exactly like AI_HEURISTIC and the game always progresses (default OFF; no network unless enabled + a seat opts in). The dispatch is an OPEN seam: AI_LLM is now wired (LlmSeat, an encapsulated HTTPRequest hook), and networked-remote (REMOTE) drops in the same way — ONE new enum value + ONE new dispatch case + a transport hook — deliberately NOT present (no stubs; the dispatcher's default branch asserts a clear error on any unwired kind, never silently passing). templates.name: Cosmic-Horror Co-op (investigation + doom, 2D board game)
templates.description: A CO-OPERATIVE cosmic-horror investigation board game (Eldritch Horror / Arkham Horror lineage — our OWN generic genre engine, no trademarked content): 1-4 INVESTIGATORS play as ONE team and race to solve MYSTERIES before DOOM consumes the world. The WHOLE rules engine is a pure, seedable, headless-testable RefCounted class (CosmicEngine) owned by a GameManager autoload, replaying byte-identically from a seed. Investigators: 8 archetypes (Scholar/Detective/Soldier/Occultist/Doctor/Reporter/Drifter/Priest), each with 5 skills (lore/influence/observation/strength/will), health + sanity pools (either at 0 -> that investigator is DEFEATED), a clue + asset inventory, and a location. World map: a graph of 9 named locations (safe towns / clue sites / gate spots) with a fixed connection layout; BFS distance drives movement. Global tracks: DOOM (starts 16 normal / 5 harsh, ticks DOWN on ominous mythos, LOST at 0) and MYSTERY progress (solve 3 to WIN); a 14-card mythos deck drives the antagonist automatically. Round structure: (1) ACTION phase — each investigator takes 2 actions from {move, rest, acquire, prepare, trade, spend_clue} (is_legal rejects non-adjacent moves / out-of-turn / defeated / unaffordable / malformed); (2) ENCOUNTER phase — each resolves a location encounter (combat / close-gate / investigate / quiet watch) via a SKILL CHECK; (3) MYTHOS phase (automated) — advance doom (scaled by a difficulty threat multiplier), open gates + spawn monsters, apply a global effect, then every monster hunts the nearest investigator. Skill checks (the core mechanic): roll a dice pool = tested skill (+ asset/focus bonuses), each die a success on 5-6, pass at >= required successes, CLUES spent to reroll failed dice — one reusable resolver resolve_from_rolls() (pure, boundary-tested) wrapped by perform_check() (seeded RNG, clue spend clamped to what you hold). Monsters: 6 types (Cultist/Deep One/Shambler/Nightgaunt/Maniac/Spawn of the Outer Dark) with distinct toughness/damage/horror/check-skill/speed; spawn from gates, move toward investigators, force combat; defeating one yields clues. Mysteries: 6 cards across 4 concrete kinds — research/ritual (invest K clues then pass a finalize check), seal (close X gates), hunt (slay a spawned quarry); solve 3 to win. LOSS: Doom 0, ALL investigators defeated, too many gates open (difficulty gate limit), or the round cap. CO-OP SEAT CONTROLLERS — all investigators are ONE team; each seat carries a ControllerKind: HUMAN_LOCAL (the dispatcher blocks for local UI input) or AI_AUTOPILOT (a GENUINE co-op heuristic ai_choose() that enumerates every legal action and scores each toward the SHARED objective — rest when a pool is critically low & safe, invest clues into the active clue-mystery (huge when the clue would COMPLETE it, holding a combat reserve otherwise), move toward the mystery goal (nearest clue site / open gate / quarry) by BFS-distance reduction, grab assets at a safe stop, bank focus before a looming encounter, hand a clue to a teammate about to finalize, stay to fight a monster on it; deterministic index tie-break, never illegal, never stalls). AI_LLM and REMOTE are DOCUMENTED FUTURE SEAMS — present as enum values, NOT wired and NOT stubbed: using one FAILS LOUD (is_supported_kind is false; the dispatcher's default branch asserts), each dropping in as ONE dispatch case + one hook. GameManager is the co-op turn dispatcher (auto-resolves autopilot seats, blocks on humans, with a 'pass the device' hand-off for LOCAL HOTSEAT), plus new_all_autopilot_game (watch the AI buddies play). board.gd renders a drawn map (locations + connections + investigator/monster tokens), a panel per investigator (skills/HP/SAN/clues/assets/location), the DOOM + MYSTERY + gate tracks, the active mystery, an action bar for the human seat, and a chronicle log — reads the engine and forwards clicks only. Full to_dict/from_dict save-load (investigators, monsters, gates, decks, mystery progress, cursor, seat controllers, RNG state). Both outcomes are genuinely reachable from the same rules — only the DIFFICULTY preset (doom start / gate limit / threat / starting vitals) changes: across 60 seeds normal won 47 / lost 13 on autopilot alone while harsh lost all 60 (Doom-0), nothing hardcoded. NoxDev ABI: buses, game_manager+persistent groups with save_data/load_data, pause+restart input, scalable_text. Replays byte-identically from a seed; drops in standalone or as the co-op investigation core of a larger game.

templates.name: Wildlife Expedition (nature-exploration + wildlife-documentation board game, 2D)
templates.description: A competitive NATURE-EXPLORATION + WILDLIFE-DOCUMENTATION board game (National-Geographic flavour — our OWN generic genre engine, no trademarks) that synthesises the SIGNATURE mechanics of three modern classics into one real depth engine: the shared exploration TRAIL + resources + SEASONS (the PARKS lineage), a SPECIES-CARD TABLEAU engine with powers + season goals (the Wingspan lineage), and BIODIVERSITY / set-variety end-game scoring (the Cascadia lineage). The WHOLE rules engine is a pure, seedable, headless-testable RefCounted class (WildlifeEngine) owned by a GameManager autoload, replaying byte-identically from a seed; 2–5 players (1 human + heuristic AIs by default). SIX tracked resources (sun/water/forest/mountain/wildlife-sighting/film) with a STRICT conservation ledger (pool == start + produced − spent, proven every turn). The shared TRAIL: 9 site tiles from a Start camp to the Trailhead, each with a biome, a resource yield and sometimes a small action (draw a species / restock gear); 2 pawns per player advance FORWARD only and good interior sites have limited CAPACITY, so the trail is a race. FOUR SEASONS: a season runs until every explorer reaches the Trailhead (or a round cap), and at each boundary season income (species + gear) fires, the active season GOAL scores, the trail RE-SEEDS (sites/bonuses shift) and pawns reset; after 4 seasons → final scoring. 37 unique SPECIES (×2 copies), each a biome, a documentation COST, a POINT value, a CATEGORY (mammal/bird/reptile/aquatic/insect/plant) and a real varied POWER (gain resources now; chain a gain whenever you later document a matching category / any species; per-season income; draw-on-rest; end-game scoring hook) — documenting plays a species to your FIELD JOURNAL (tableau) and fires its power AND any chain powers already in the journal. An OBSERVATION gate ties documenting to where your pawns stand (a pawn must observe the species' biome; sightings are the observation currency, discounted by Binoculars gear). FOUR season goals (one active per season) + SEVEN EXPEDITION contracts (end-game bonus objectives) + 6 developable GEAR/stations (points + an ongoing perk). SET-VARIETY SCORING: escalating tiers for distinct CATEGORIES and distinct BIOMES, a largest-single-category bonus, per-species points, gear, expeditions and accumulated season goals — final_scoring() yields a per-player breakdown whose COMPONENTS SUM to the total, with a single deterministic winner. A turn is exactly ONE legal action from {MOVE, DOCUMENT, REST, DEVELOP}: is_legal() rejects out-of-turn / finished / move-backward / occupied-site / off-biome / unaffordable / malformed actions and apply_action() re-validates. The AI is a genuine NON-LLM heuristic (ai_choose): it enumerates every legal action and scores each by species value + power synergy + progress toward goals & expeditions + resource efficiency + diversity gain + trail-yield value, deterministic index tie-break, never illegal, never stalls. PLAY MODES — a SEAT-CONTROLLER matrix: every 2–5 seats carries a ControllerKind — HUMAN_LOCAL (the dispatcher blocks for local UI input) or AI_HEURISTIC (auto-resolves via ai_choose) — and GameManager is the turn dispatcher. templates.name: Falling Sand (cellular-automata physics sandbox, 2D)
templates.description: A falling-sand physics SANDBOX (Noita / The Powder Toy / Sandspiel lineage) - a cellular-automata world where every cell is a MATERIAL that obeys deterministic physics rules and the player paints materials with a brush and watches emergent chemistry. The whole simulation is SandWorld (a pure, seedable, RefCounted GDScript engine, no scene deps): a WxH grid in packed arrays (_cells material ids + _aux per-cell state), 14 MATERIALS with distinct behaviour (empty, sand/ash powders, water/oil/acid/lava liquids with a density model so fluids separate, stone/wood/plant/ice solids, fire, smoke, steam), and 11 real REACTIONS (water+lava->stone+steam; fire ignites wood/oil/plant, fuel consumed; lava ignites flammables and cools to stone; fire burns out->ash/empty+smoke; acid dissolves a solid, both consumed; watered plant grows; steam condenses->water; ice melts near heat->water). The step() is a pure function of (grid, tick, seeded RNG): a bottom->top scan with per-tick parity direction + a _moved mask means every cell updates at most once (bounded O(W*H), no infinite loops), and all randomness comes from a seeded RNG whose state is saved - so the same seed + same scripted brush inputs give a BYTE-IDENTICAL grid after N steps. paint(material,x,y,radius) stamps a disc; snapshot()/restore() round-trip the whole grid + RNG state. main.gd renders the grid one-pixel-per-cell to an ImageTexture on a TextureRect, stepped on the physics tick, with a material palette, mouse-drag painting, a brush-size stepper, and pause/step/clear. NoxDev ABI: buses, game_manager+persistent groups with save_data/load_data (whole grid + RNG persist), pause + restart input, scalable_text. Drops in standalone or as the physics layer of a larger game.

templates.name: God Game (top-down deity strategy sim, 2D)
templates.description: A top-down DEITY strategy sim (Populous / Black & White lineage) - shape the terrain and wield divine powers to grow your tribe of autonomous followers and outgrow, convert, or drown a rival god's tribe. The whole game is GodWorld (a pure, seedable, RefCounted GDScript engine, no scene deps): a 64x64 terrain grid in packed arrays (_height per cell - water below sea level, walkable/buildable land above - plus a resource flag: forest=wood, food source=food), deterministically generated from the seed (noise -> box-blur hills -> scattered resources) with two founding settlements. Two TRIBES (you + a rival) each have villagers, huts, a belief/mana pool and a wood pool; belief accrues each tick from congregation size. A real autonomous populace AI (deterministic): followers seek the nearest resource, fell forests for wood + forage food back to the hut, huts BREED new followers when fed, the tribe BUILDS new huts on free buildable land near resources (funded expansion - it gathers wood first then raises one hut at a time), and followers FLEE flooded cells (and drown if stranded). 5 divine POWERS with real effects: RAISE_LAND (water -> buildable land / land-bridges), LOWER_LAND (flood land, drown/scatter followers, wash away huts), GROW_FOOD (bless food sources + a bounty to your hut -> attracts + breeds your tribe), INSPIRE (boost your followers' gather -> faster building + breeding), MIRACLE (convert nearby rival followers to your tribe); each costs belief and is_legal() rejects a cast with no belief or on an invalid target. A rival god AI (deterministic heuristic, non-LLM) accrues belief and spends it on a fixed priority ladder (convert a stray follower of yours, grow itself with food, flood your nearest settlement) with deterministic tie-breaks. tick_world() is a pure function of (state, tick, seeded RNG): accrue belief -> apply queued player powers -> rival decides+casts -> advance every villager one cell in index order -> advance huts (flood+breed) -> compact the dead -> judge; no unbounded rescans (bounded-radius resource search only when idle), so a tick is O(V*R^2 + W*H) and always terminates. Win/loss is genuinely reachable both ways: WIN by hitting the population goal while leading or eliminating the rival; LOSE by being wiped out or letting the rival hit the goal first; the tick cap awards the larger tribe - the same seed + same scripted divine commands give a BYTE-IDENTICAL world after N ticks. snapshot()/restore() round-trip the ENTIRE world (terrain + both tribes + belief/wood + pending powers + RNG state); checksum() proves determinism. main.gd renders the grid top-down (height->colour, water, forests/food, per-tribe villager+hut markers, inspired followers glow), with a power palette (pick a power, click the map to cast on a cell), a belief+wood meter, both populations, a tick counter and a log. NoxDev ABI: buses, game_manager+persistent groups with save_data/load_data (whole world + RNG persist), pause + restart input, scalable_text. Drops in standalone or as the strategy layer of a larger game.

templates.name: Poker Roguelike (Balatro-style deck-scoring roguelike, 2D)
templates.description: A Balatro-lineage POKER-SCORING ROGUELIKE: draw a hand, play a 1-5 card poker hand to beat an escalating score TARGET, and warp the math with JOKERS + planet-style hand-level upgrades bought in a shop. OUR OWN engine, generic content (no trademarks) -- a PURE, seedable, deterministic scoring engine. PokerEngine is a pure RefCounted class (no Godot-node dependency) so a run replays BYTE-IDENTICALLY from a seed and drives headlessly. It does REAL poker hand detection for all 12 types (high-card..straight-flush plus five-of-a-kind/flush-house/flush-five, wheel + ace-high straights) and EXACT deterministic scoring: score = (base_chips[type][level] + scored-card chips) x (base_mult[type][level] + card mult), then every joker applies IN SLOT ORDER (+chips/+mult/xmult, conditionally), then chips x mult rounded half-up -- score_breakdown() exposes every component. 25 jokers with varied real effects (flat, conditional-on-hand-contains, per-held-card, per-discard, per-joker, retrigger, and scaling jokers that grow). Run structure: antes 1..8 x small/big/boss blinds with escalating targets, N hands + M discards per blind, a shop selling jokers + hand-level upgrades for money (blind reward + interest); WIN = clear ante 8's boss, LOSE = miss a blind. A deterministic auto-play heuristic (best_play/auto_take_turn) drives a full solo run. is_legal rejects illegal plays; to_dict/from_dict save the whole run incl. RNG. GameManager autoload adds the NoxDev ABI (buses, game_manager+persistent groups with save_data/load_data, pause+restart input, scalable_text). table.gd renders the play surface in code -- ante/blind/target + running score, money + hands/discards, the selectable hand, joker slots, Play/Discard, a shop, and an Auto Step demo. templates.name: Video Store Sim (80s VHS-rental-store management sim, 2D)
templates.description: An 80s VHS RENTAL-STORE management sim (neon-Blockbuster / retail-tycoon lineage): you OWN the store - a CATALOG of VHS TITLES across six genres (action/comedy/horror/drama/family/scifi) that you BUY COPIES of - and grow your NET WORTH by stocking the right depth of hot NEW RELEASES vs the evergreen catalogue. THE HEART is NEW-RELEASE HYPE - a title’s rental DEMAND is a pure time function demand(t,day)=baseline+baseline*gain*e^(-lambda*weeks): a new release SPIKES the week it drops then DECAYS exponentially over the weeks toward its catalogue baseline, so how many copies of a hot release to stock vs catalogue is the core daily decision. A real CUSTOMER ECONOMY: each day seeded FOOT TRAFFIC = base x reputation x marketing x selection-breadth x membership x noise, staff THROUGHPUT caps how many are served, each served customer wants a title (weighted by current demand) and RENTS an available copy (a new-release premium price) - or if every copy is out it is a MISSED rental (lost goodwill). RETURNS after a few days, some LATE (LATE FEES, a real revenue stream, but a harsh late-fee policy CHURNS members), a few DAMAGED (the tape leaves stock). A cash-flow ECONOMY with strict INTEGER MONEY CONSERVATION (cash == start + sum of a named-flow ledger) verified EVERY day, tracking CASH + NET WORTH (cash + depreciated inventory - debt); MEMBERSHIP (happy renters sign up -> members multiply traffic; bad service churns them); is_legal-gated ACTIONS (buy_copies/remove_copy/set_staff/hire/set_late_fee/marketing/loan/repay) that reject illegal moves with byte-identical state; days->months with loan interest; a WIN (reach the net-worth goal) and a LOSS (bankruptcy) both genuinely reachable by a deterministic auto-play, with a MAX_DAYS cap that guarantees termination; and full save/load incl. RNG (byte-identical replays, FNV-1a state checksum stable across processes). store.gd renders a HUD (cash/net worth/reputation/members/day-month/traffic+income), a scrollable CATALOG (title/genre/current demand+new-release flag/copies owned-shelf-out/today rentals vs misses), an action panel (title picker + quantity slider to buy copies, late-fee slider, marketing/hire/fire/loan, next-day + auto-play) and a finance readout - all in code. NoxDev ABI: buses, game_manager+persistent groups with save_data/load_data (whole store persists), pause + restart input, scalable_text. templates.name: Ant Colony (colony ecosystem sim, 2D)
templates.description: A SimAnt-style COLONY ECOSYSTEM sim - grow an ant colony via pheromone-trail foraging, tunnel out a nest, raise castes, and war with a rival colony while dodging a spider predator. A deterministic tick-sim, sibling to falling-sand + god-game. The whole game is AntWorld (a pure, seedable, RefCounted GDScript engine, no scene deps): a 56x40 grid with an open SURFACE band over diggable SOIL (packed arrays: terrain kind + per-cell food), bedrock veins, food sources on the ground line, and two nests carved just below the surface, all generated deterministically from the seed. The SIGNATURE mechanic is TWIN PHEROMONE fields per colony (float grids): a HOME trail emitted by the nest core each tick (and laid weakly by outbound searchers) and a FOOD trail laid by food-carriers back to the nest; both EVAPORATE (x0.94) and DIFFUSE through connected passable cells every tick, so an unused trail fades and a used one is reinforced - a stable nest->food trail EMERGES with no scripting (searchers ascend the food trail to reach food, carriers ascend the home trail to get home). CASTES: a QUEEN (lays eggs, consumes food, never moves - kill her to win), WORKERS (forage + dig, follow/lay pheromones), SOLDIERS (fight rival ants + the predator); ants are a deterministic state machine (search->return->dig, or guard->fight). A real colony ECONOMY: foraged food banks into a stock, the queen turns food into eggs->new ants (every 5 ticks, one egg costs 5 food, pop cap 70, ~1-in-3 soldiers), upkeep drains food and a persistently empty larder starves (and kills) the queen. TUNNELLING: idle workers excavate SOIL->TUNNEL at the frontier nearest the nest (or your Dig zone), expanding the nest (<=3 diggers at once). A RIVAL colony runs the same ant AI plus an aggressive heuristic - its soldiers relentlessly march on YOUR queen, so a passive player is overrun. A spider PREDATOR stalks the surface, bites lone workers, is damaged by adjacent soldiers, and is driven off at zero hp (respawning later). Indirect SimAnt-style player influence: designate DIG / FORAGE / ATTACK zones that bias the colony; is_legal_designation() rejects out-of-bounds/bad-kind. tick_world() is a pure function of (state, tick, seeded RNG): evaporate+diffuse pheromones -> nests emit home scent -> economy -> assign a digger -> advance every ant one cell in index order -> advance predators -> compact the dead -> judge; no unbounded rescans, so a tick is O(A + W*H) and always terminates. Win/loss is genuinely reachable both ways: WIN by killing the rival queen (or wiping their ants, or leading at the pop goal); LOSE if your queen dies or your colony hits zero ants; the tick cap awards the larger colony - the same seed + same designations give a BYTE-IDENTICAL world after N ticks. snapshot()/restore() round-trip the ENTIRE world (terrain + food + both colonies' ants + pools + BOTH pheromone fields + zones + RNG state); checksum() proves determinism. main.gd renders the grid top-down (surface/soil/tunnels/chambers, food, both nests, ants coloured by colony+caste with the queen large and carriers haloed, the spider, an optional food-pheromone heat overlay), with a zone palette (pick Dig/Forage/Attack, click the map to designate), a HUD (your pop by caste, food, foraged total, tunnels, rival pop, tick) and a log. NoxDev ABI: buses, game_manager+persistent groups with save_data/load_data (whole world + pheromones + RNG persist), pause + restart input, scalable_text. Drops in standalone or as the colony-sim layer of a larger game.

KEY DESIGN DECISION: the ball-vs-peg physics is a DETERMINISTIC custom fixed-timestep circle sim, NOT Godot's RigidBody2D (whose solver is not identical across runs/builds and would break byte-identical replays). A ball is a circle (position, velocity) advanced at a fixed dt under gravity, colliding with circular PEGS + the four WALLS by pure geometry (circle-circle / circle-wall overlap -> push out + reflect the velocity about the contact normal with a restitution factor) until it exits the bottom or hits a MAX_STEPS cap -- so given (aim angle, board layout, seed) the trajectory + accumulated damage are 100% reproducible; the only randomness (board/map gen, deck shuffle, shop rolls, enemy stats, events) is one seeded RNG saved with the run, the physics has zero randomness. PegEngine is a pure RefCounted class (no Godot-node dependency) so a whole run replays byte-identically + drives headlessly. 4 peg types (normal / crit / bomb-AoE / refresh); a DECK of 14 distinct orbs (crit-boost, multiball, poison, per-remaining-peg, momentum, echo x2, bomb-boost, pierce, heal, gold, refresh-synergy, plain) drawn/discarded with reshuffle; combat vs an enemy with HP + a cycling attack pattern + status effects (poison ticks); a seeded connected node MAP with 10 RELICS, gold, orb rewards + a shop. is_legal rejects illegal actions; to_dict/from_dict save the whole run incl. the peg board + RNG; a deterministic auto-play (best_aim / auto_take_turn) drives a full run to WIN (clear the boss) or LOSE (HP to 0). A top is a circle (position, velocity, plus WEIGHT and SPIN which encodes STAMINA + a spin DIRECTION) advanced at a fixed dt inside a circular bowl; motion is a pure deterministic sum of a bowl slope (a centre spring), passive friction, an aggression wander (a deterministic sine of the top's age, NOT an RNG), and a seek toward the nearest opponent; collisions are pure circle-circle geometry (overlap -> weighted elastic momentum transfer along the contact normal + an attack knockback + a stamina drain scaled by attacker.attack vs defender.defense and the same-spin/opposite-spin interaction). templates.name: Martial Arts Brawler (Jade-Empire-style 2D beat-'em-up RPG)
templates.description: A JADE-EMPIRE-lineage 2D BEAT-'EM-UP MARTIAL-ARTS RPG: learn martial-arts STYLES from masters of different cultures, SWITCH STYLES mid-fight to exploit a rock-paper-scissors matchup triangle, and grow a martial-artist through a bounded CAMPAIGN of escalating encounters ending in a final duel. OUR OWN engine, generic content (no trademarks). KEY DESIGN DECISION: the combat is a DETERMINISTIC custom fixed-timestep 1-D-stage sim, NOT Godot CharacterBody2D/RigidBody2D (whose solver + frame scheduler are not identical across runs/builds and would break byte-identical replays). Each fighter is a POINT on a horizontal stage (x, velocity, facing, HP, CHI, a current STYLE, an action state idle/attacking/block/blockstun/hitstun) advanced at a fixed dt (1/60): resolve each side's input (a human action or an AI policy) -> start an ACTION with real startup/active/recovery FRAME DATA; ACTIVE frames project a HITBOX interval in front of the fighter (a melee reach or a travelling chi projectile); a hitbox overlapping the foe's HURTBOX applies damage scaled by the move + the attacker's attributes + a STYLE MATCHUP multiplier + technique upgrades, plus knockback + hitstun; blocking converts the hit to chip + blockstun. The combat has ZERO randomness; the only RNG is one seeded generator for optional AI decision jitter (config ai_jitter, default 0) saved with the run, so given (seed, config, input/AI script) a fight is 100% reproducible + byte-identical across processes; MAX_STEPS bounds every fight. SIX styles from distinct cultures/archetypes (Drunken Fist=fast, Iron Ox=power, Willow Guard=defensive, Ghost Palm=ranged chi projectile, Steel Crane=weapon reach, Coiling Serpent=grappling throws), each a base-stat modifier + a three-move set (light/heavy/special) of genuine frame data. A STYLE MATCHUP triangle scales damage (advantaged x1.40, disadvantaged x0.70) with the core fast>power>defensive>fast holding exactly and three consistent extra spokes (no mutual contradictions), so switching to the right counter measurably wins faster -- not cosmetic. You start knowing two styles; each master teaches one more (learning unlocks its moves); technique points deepen a known style (+10% move damage each). Three attributes (Body/Mind/Spirit) scale HP/chi/damage; XP -> level -> attribute + technique points; a finite ramping campaign of six encounters ends in a final duel. WIN = clear the final duel; LOSE = burn all continues; BOTH reachable by a deterministic auto-play (run_campaign wins on the base difficulty + loses on a buffed one) and the campaign always terminates. BrawlerEngine is a pure RefCounted class (no Godot-node dependency) so the whole campaign replays byte-identically + drives headlessly. is_legal rejects illegal actions (unlearned move/style, acting mid-recovery); to_dict/from_dict + run_checksum save the whole run incl. learned styles + progression + the RNG and prove determinism. GameManager autoload adds the NoxDev ABI (buses, game_manager+persistent groups with save_data/load_data, pause+restart input, scalable_text). arena.gd renders the play surface in code -- the stage + two fighters (markers with HP + chi bars + the active hitbox) via _draw(), a fixed-timestep combat accumulator, the human input (light/heavy/special/guard/walk + a mid-fight style switch), a style/level HUD, a combat log, and a learn/technique mastery panel, plus Auto-Fight + Auto Campaign demos. templates.name: Pirate Career Sim (age-of-sail, systemic)
templates.description: An age-of-sail PIRATE CAREER SIM (Sid Meier’s Pirates! lineage) with DEEPER, fully-systemic mechanics. KEY DESIGN DECISION: the whole career is a pure, seedable, headless-testable RefCounted class (PirateEngine) on a deterministic DAY-TICK model — NO physics nodes — so every price, wind, broadside, boarding roll, mutiny check and rival move is a pure function of state + ONE seeded RNG, and a career replays BYTE-IDENTICALLY from a seed (verified across processes). A MAX_CAREER_DAYS cap + “every action costs ≥1 day” bounds it, so it ALWAYS terminates in a WIN (retire at/above a rank) or a LOSS (ship sunk with no reserves / crew mutiny / retire below threshold) — no hardcoded winner. TEN interlocking systems, all real formulas: WORLD (16 ports across 4 rival nations Crown/Empire/Republic/Company, each with position, owner, wealth tier, garrison + a per-good local economy); SAILING (travel costs days via a deterministic wind model — downwind fast, upwind slow, navigation eases it — that also sets sea-combat initiative/the weather gauge); TRADE (6 goods sugar/tobacco/cloth/rum/spice/ivory priced on a real supply/demand scarcity curve; buying/selling MOVES the local price unit-by-unit (price impact) and prices DRIFT back to equilibrium, so cross-port arbitrage is emergent + profitable, never scripted); SEA COMBAT (deterministic turn-based ship duel — maneuver for the wind gauge, fire broadsides scaling with cannons × gunnery × range × hull, pick round/chain/grape shot to wreck hull/sails/crew → sink/flee/board, turn-capped); BOARDING (crew-vs-crew melee from crew × morale × captain fencing → capture ship+cargo or get repelled); NATION REPUTATION (a 4-nation standing vector — attacking a nation lowers its standing + lifts its sworn enemy’s; Letters of Marque sanction privateering; very low standing makes ports hostile + spawns warship hunters); CREW & MORALE (wages, food, plunder shares; low morale → MUTINY ends the career; dividing plunder + shore leave restore it); templates.name: Ragdoll Locomotion (QWOP-style physics-comedy walker + MP)
templates.description: A QWOP-lineage RAGDOLL LOCOMOTION game: actuate an athlete's individual muscles (four groups mapped to Q/W/O/P, each an antagonistic joint pair) to stagger an articulated body FORWARD as far as possible before it topples. Reach the goal distance to WIN; faceplant (head/shoulder hits the floor, or the torso tips past a threshold) or time out to LOSE. KEY DESIGN DECISION: the articulated-body physics is a DETERMINISTIC custom fixed-timestep sim, NOT Godot's RigidBody2D (whose solver is not identical across runs/builds and would break byte-identical replays + probes). RagdollEngine is a pure RefCounted class (no Godot-node dependency): the athlete is 11 point masses wired by 10 rigid BONE distance-constraints (>=7 rigid segments -- head, torso, thighL/shinL/footL, thighR/shinR/footR, upperArm, lowerArm); each fixed step (DT=1/120) integrates velocities under gravity + air drag (semi-implicit Euler), integrates positions, then runs a fixed 10-pass XPBD-style relaxation (rigidify bones -> drive MUSCLE angular-constraints toward the player's target angles -> a torso balance reflex -> resolve GROUND contact + Coulomb friction) and re-derives velocities from the projected motion so a constraint can never inject energy (no NaN, no explosion); a per-node speed clamp + bone-reconciliation pass means the athlete must genuinely WALK, never teleport/slide. A planted foot GRIPS the floor (traction), so a hip that extends against a gripped foot levers the torso forward -- real walking, not skating. The physics has ZERO randomness (the only RNG is a seeded start-jitter saved with the run); MAX_STEPS bounds every run -> a timeout, never an infinite stagger. Given (seed, config, per-frame muscle inputs) the trajectory is 100% reproducible and byte-identical across separate processes. Three difficulty presets (easy walks to a 6 m goal, normal is stable to the full 100 m, hard is a weak-balance teeter); set_muscle(i,on) drives the joint targets for live human control; deterministic policy_walk (advances a positive distance + clears the easy goal) and policy_fall (topples the athlete) prove WIN and LOSS are reachable; to_dict/from_dict + run_checksum save the whole run incl. the RNG and prove determinism. It ships the nox_netcode realtime drop-in PRE-WIRED (same _net_active() seam as obby-multiplayer): OFFLINE it drives one local athlete through GameManager's engine and renders it (a complete single-player QWOP game, byte-identical whether or not the netcode nodes are present); ONLINE (a session running) the NetSpawner spawns one net_athlete per peer via MultiplayerSpawner+MultiplayerSynchronizer (each peer sims its OWN athlete + syncs its body POSE) and NetEvents arbitrates the authoritative finish order -- a ragdoll RACE. Authority is per-peer over its own athlete (the exact obby net_player seam -- authority from the node name at spawn, owner-drives/others-render -- only the replicated payload is an articulated pose, no new protocol). GameManager autoload adds the NoxDev ABI (buses, game_manager+persistent groups with save_data/load_data, pause+restart input, scalable_text). track.gd renders the play surface in code -- the athlete (code-drawn bones + joints), the scrolling ground with metre markers + a goal line, and a distance HUD -- with Q/W/O/P muscle input. Drops in standalone or as the locomotion/traversal core of a larger game.

templates.name: Department Store Sim (80s big-box department-store & mail-order sim, 2D)
templates.description: An 80s BIG-BOX DEPARTMENT-STORE management sim (Kmart / Sears-of-the-catalog-era; sibling of mall-tycoon and video-store-sim, sharing their deterministic integer-money economy and money-conservation discipline, but DISTINCT: a multi-department retailer with a MAIL-ORDER CATALOGUE channel and SEASONAL demand - not a mall of leased units, not a single-category rental shop). You OWN the store - EIGHT DEPARTMENTS (apparel/electronics/toys/appliances/home&garden/automotive/jewelry/sporting-goods), 4 PRODUCT LINES each (32 lines), each line with a per-unit cost + shelf price (margin) + baseline demand, each department with its own STAFFING, FLOOR-SPACE allocation and SEASONAL profile. The whole sim is a pure, seedable, headless-testable DeptStoreEngine (RefCounted) owned by a GameManager autoload. TWO DISTINCTIVE SYSTEMS: (1) SEASONAL DEMAND as a real COMPUTED curve (not a table) - each department's demand is a pure function of the day-of-year, a sum of wrapped-Gaussian season bumps season(dept,day)=1+sum amp*e^(-dist(doy,center)^2/(2*width^2)): toys/electronics SPIKE at Christmas, home&garden peaks in spring, jewelry at Valentine/Christmas, automotive/sporting in summer; (2) the MAIL-ORDER CATALOGUE (the Sears book) - PUBLISH a seasonal catalogue (an up-front print cost) to open a SECOND demand stream that reaches buyers the physical floor never sees (rural/remote - NOT gated by store foot traffic or floor staff), drawing from the SAME shared inventory, RESERVING stock into transit and SHIPPING after a LEAD TIME (cash lands later) with a per-order fulfillment fee - a real trade-off of print+fulfillment vs incremental reach. A real CUSTOMER ECONOMY: each day seeded FOOT TRAFFIC = base x reputation x marketing x overall-season x staff-presence x noise; each customer visits a DEPARTMENT (weighted by its lines' demand x its floor space), per-department STAFF THROUGHPUT caps how many are served (rest TURNED AWAY), a served customer BUYS if the line has stock (-> revenue, on-hand consumed) or MISSES on a STOCKOUT (lost sale + goodwill). MARKDOWNS/CLEARANCE: unsold seasonal stock AGES; a per-line markdown cuts price (recovering cash but slashing margin) AND lifts demand pull, clearing aging inventory (plus a liquidate-to-jobber salvage lever). A cash-flow ECONOMY with strict INTEGER MONEY CONSERVATION (cash == start + sum of a named-flow ledger) verified EVERY day, tracking CASH + NET WORTH (cash + depreciated inventory - debt), with a UNIT INVENTORY INVARIANT purchased == on_hand + in_transit + consumed holding per line every day; reputation moves with in-stock rate, service (staffing) and fair pricing. is_legal-gated ACTIONS (restock/liquidate/set_markdown/set_dept_staff/hire/set_dept_space/publish_catalogue/marketing/loan/repay) reject illegal moves with byte-identical state; days->months with loan interest; a WIN (net-worth goal) and a LOSS (bankruptcy) both genuinely reachable by a deterministic auto-play, with a MAX_DAYS cap that guarantees termination; and full save/load incl. RNG (byte-identical replays, FNV-1a state checksum stable across processes). store_floor.gd renders a HUD (cash/net worth/reputation/day-month+season/traffic+income), a per-department panel, a per-line list of the selected department, an action panel (department+line pickers, restock quantity slider, markdown slider, hire/space +/-, publish-catalogue/marketing/loan, next-day + auto-play) and a finance readout - all in code. NoxDev ABI: buses, game_manager+persistent groups with save_data/load_data (whole store persists), pause + restart input, scalable_text. A deterministic economy/inventory engine mirroring mall-tycoon's and video-store-sim's money-conservation discipline - swap the DEPARTMENT + PRODUCT-LINE catalogues and SEASON_BUMPS for your own.

templates.name: Animal Society RPG (survival + migration, 2D)
templates.description: A mature ANIMAL-SOCIETY survival RPG in the WATERSHIP DOWN / SECRET OF NIMH / AMERICAN TAIL lineage - lead a small band of NAMED animals (rabbits / mice) with distinct SOCIAL ROLES across a dangerous seeded landscape to found and grow a THRIVING WARREN, surviving predators, seasons, hunger, and the band's own morale. A deterministic day/tick society + survival sim, sibling to ant-colony + god-game + cosmic-horror-coop. The whole game is WarrenEngine (a pure, seedable, RefCounted GDScript engine, no scene deps) replaying byte-identically from a seed. THE BAND: a colony of named animals, each with a SOCIAL ROLE (Chief/leader, Scout, Seer/lookout, Forager, Fighter/protector, Storyteller/morale, Kit/young), a sex, an age, traits (courage/wisdom/speed) + health, needs (hunger/fatigue), and a bond to the band; roles confer REAL, measurable abilities - a Forager gathers strictly more food, a Scout lowers the ambush rate, a Seer's early-warning improves escape in a raid, a Fighter raises the band's defence, a Storyteller lifts morale on rest, a Chief steadies morale + reduces dissent, and a Kit matures into an adult role. SURVIVAL SIM: seasons cycle over a year and modulate food + danger (lean, dangerous winter vs rich, calm summer); each day the band takes ONE decision, then the day RESOLVES - everyone eats (starvation harms/kills), fatigue + illness accrue, a hazard may strike, morale drifts, and once settled a breeding pair reproduces up to a population cap. PREDATORS + HAZARDS: four threats (Fox / Hawk / Cat / the “Man” and his road and machines) hunt on a deterministic model - a Scout/Seer warning + the band's fighters/speed decide escape vs loss, and a raid can KILL a named member (real stakes). SOCIAL LAYER (the Watership heart): morale/cohesion is driven by leadership, storytelling, losses, and success; low morale -> dissent -> a member may DESERT and splinter the band. MIGRATION QUEST: the band travels a seeded chain of stops (from the old Sandleford warren toward Watership Down); each leg costs days + risks a predator encounter, and you must arrive with a viable founding group (enough survivors + a breeding pair) then GROW the warren to a target size. take_action() applies one decision then advances the day via _end_day() (a MOVE_ON runs one _end_day per travel-day of the leg): eat -> fatigue/illness -> hazard -> morale drift -> reproduction -> aging/maturation -> desertion -> compact the fallen -> judge; no unbounded rescans, so every run terminates under MAX_DAYS. Win/loss is genuinely reachable both ways under a deterministic auto-play policy: WIN by founding the new warren AND growing it to the target (a thriving society); LOSE if the band is wiped out, falls below a viable founding size, or fails to reach the site before the deadline. Forage yield, encounter chance, and rest-morale gain are exposed as pure preview helpers so the role abilities are directly, measurably testable. The balanced policy plays to survive + thrive (forage when lean, rest for morale, scout a dangerous leg, then move on; in growth it keeps a surplus so the pair breeds), the reckless policy charges on and gets the band killed - nothing hardcoded (across 60 seeds balanced won 54 / lost 6, reckless lost all 60). to_dict()/from_dict() round-trip the ENTIRE run (the band + roles/sexes/ages/traits/needs/bonds + morale + food + journey + counters + RNG state); checksum() (FNV-1a) proves determinism within a process AND across two separate processes. warren.gd renders a drawn journey strip (stops, the band's position, danger shaded, the goal, a role-coloured band token), a HUD (season/year/day/food/morale/population/phase/cohesion), a decision bar (Forage/Scout/Rest/Move On/Shelter/Assign Role), and a members panel - reads the engine and forwards a decision only. NoxDev ABI: buses, game_manager+persistent groups with save_data/load_data (whole run + RNG persist), pause + restart input, scalable_text. Drops in standalone or as the survival/society layer of a larger game.

templates.name: Farm Management Sim (whole-farm operation & commodity economy sim, 2D)
templates.description: A deeper redo of Maxis's SIMFARM: you run a whole farm BUSINESS from a top-down desk - fields, crop rotation, soil chemistry, irrigation, weather, livestock, commodity markets and machinery - as a deterministic integer-money economy sim. Sibling of dept-store-sim and mall-tycoon (sharing their money-conservation discipline and seasonal wrapped-Gaussian curves) but DISTINCT: an agronomy + commodity operation, not a retailer; and DISTINCT from the existing farming-sim (a hands-on 2D top-down crop-tending game) - this is the top-down MANAGEMENT/economy sim of the whole farm operation. The whole sim is a pure, seedable, headless-testable FarmEngine (RefCounted): 9 FIELDS (a 3x3 grid) each with soil quality + a live soil NITROGEN level; 6 CROPS (corn/wheat/soybeans/cotton/hay/vegetables) each with a growth duration, water need, plantable seasons, a nitrogen DRAW (or, for the soybean legume, a nitrogen FIX), a seed cost and a yield curve. THREE DISTINCTIVE SYSTEMS: (1) SOIL + CROP ROTATION as a real per-field NITROGEN BALANCE - harvesting a heavy-feeder DRAWS nitrogen down so a MONOCULTURE measurably DEPLETES the field and its yields DECLINE, while rotating in the nitrogen-FIXING legume, applying FERTILIZER or resting the field REPLENISHES it and yields recover (nutrient_factor(N) folds straight into yield); (2) DETERMINISTIC WEATHER + four SEASONS as a PURE HASH of (seed,day) independent of the livestock RNG stream - rain/drought/frost/heat/pest events reproducible and testable, weather modulating each field's daily growth condition and DAMAGING crops (frost hits warm-season crops, pests cut yield), with IRRIGATION (a per-field daily cost) buying back the water a drought takes; (3) a COMMODITY MARKET as a real COMPUTED price curve base*(1+sum amp*sin(2pi*day/period+phase)) with a seed-derived phase per commodity - a genuine multi-frequency wave, not a table, so a commodity's price VARIES over the year and sell-timing changes revenue. LIVESTOCK: three herds (cattle->milk, pigs->meat, chickens->eggs) that eat FEED (hay you grow or feed you buy), produce daily goods into commodity stock, BREED when well-fed (bounded by a per-type cap) and face daily MORTALITY (higher when underfed) resolved by the seeded RNG. MACHINERY: tractors + harvesters raise field THROUGHPUT and HARVEST EFFICIENCY and trim labour at a purchase + maintenance + DEPRECIATION cost. A cash-flow ECONOMY with strict INTEGER MONEY CONSERVATION (cash == start + sum of a named-flow ledger via _apply_cash) verified EVERY day, tracking CASH + NET WORTH (cash + land + depreciated machinery + stock + herds - debt); is_legal-gated ACTIONS (plant/harvest/fertilize/set_irrigation/buy+sell_livestock/buy_feed/sell_commodity/buy_machinery/loan/repay) reject illegal moves with byte-identical state; days->months with loan interest; a WIN (net-worth goal) and a LOSS (bankruptcy/foreclosure) both genuinely reachable by a deterministic auto-play, with a MAX (max_years*360) cap that guarantees termination; and full save/load incl. RNG (byte-identical replays, FNV-1a state checksum stable across processes). farm.gd renders the 3x3 FIELD GRID via _draw (each cell coloured by crop with a nitrogen-shaded soil tint, irrigation border, maturity ring, projected-yield text), a HUD (cash/net worth/day-season-year/weather/work capacity), a commodity-market panel, a herds+machinery+finance readout and an action panel (field/crop/animal/commodity pickers, a quantity slider, plant/harvest/fertilize/irrigate/buy+sell/machinery/loan + next-day + auto-play) - all in code. NoxDev ABI: buses, game_manager+persistent groups with save_data/load_data (whole farm persists), pause + restart input, scalable_text. A deterministic agronomy/commodity engine mirroring dept-store-sim's and mall-tycoon's money-conservation discipline - swap the CROP + LIVESTOCK + MACHINERY + COMMODITY catalogues and the weather/season/price-wave parameters for your own.

templates.name: Classic Roguelike (Rogue/NetHack-lineage turn-based dungeon crawl, 2D)
templates.description: A Rogue/NetHack-lineage CLASSIC ROGUELIKE: descend a procedurally generated multi-level dungeon one turn at a time, fight monsters with bump-combat, grab gold and potions, level up, and try to reach the bottom before permadeath ends the run. OUR OWN engine, generic content (no trademarks) -- RogueEngine is a PURE, seedable RefCounted class (no Godot-node dependency, no Time calls: a single private RNG drives world-gen AND play) so a run replays BYTE-IDENTICALLY from a seed and drives headlessly. Real systems: seeded dungeon generation (non-overlapping rooms joined by L-shaped corridors, guaranteed-connected, down-stairs placed each floor), turn-based bump-combat (move into a monster to attack; monsters take their turn after you), a 4-tier monster table (rat/kobold/orc/wraith) that deepens with depth, greedy monster AI, fog-of-war exploration (seen[] tiles), items (gold + healing potions), 8-floor descent with escalating danger, XP/level-up (raises max HP + attack), and PERMADEATH (death or reaching MAX_DEPTH ends the run). checksum() is an FNV-1a fold over the entire state (grid + player + monsters + items) for cross-process determinism proof. A deterministic BFS-pathfinding auto-play seat (auto_step/auto_play_to_end 'greedy') clears reachable monsters then routes to the stairs and descends -- it plays a real run to a genuine win/death (validated: a seeded run reaches depth 5 and dies at turn 303), not a stalled loop. save_data/load_data snapshot the whole run (NoxDev ABI). GameManager autoload wires the ABI (buses, new_run reseed, save/load); dungeon.gd renders the crawl in code -- fog-lit tiles, the @ player, monsters, gold/potion pips, a live HUD (depth/HP/ATK/level/gold/potions) and the message log, with arrow/WASD movement, Q to quaff, > to descend, R for a fresh seeded run. Drops in standalone or as the dungeon core of a larger RPG.

templates.name: Real-Time Strategy (StarCraft/AoE-lite base-build + army, 2D)
templates.description: A StarCraft/AoE-lineage REAL-TIME STRATEGY: mine minerals with workers, spend them to train more workers + a barracks + soldiers, and raze the enemy town hall before yours falls. OUR OWN engine, generic content (no trademarks). The trick that keeps an RTS in the NoxDev pure-engine pattern: RtsEngine runs as a DETERMINISTIC FIXED-TIMESTEP LOCKSTEP sim -- play advances in discrete ticks, commands are queued and applied in a fixed sorted order, and a single private RNG drives world-gen + tie-breaks + AI, so a whole match replays BYTE-IDENTICALLY from a seed and drives headlessly (no Godot-node or Time dependency in the engine). Real systems: two symmetric bases (town hall + starting workers + a 4-patch mineral field, with seeded opening jitter); a worker economy (walk to patch -> mine a load over MINE_TICKS -> haul CARRY back to a town hall -> deposit, auto-resuming the nearest live patch); production queues (town hall trains workers, barracks trains soldiers, one at a time, paid on queue); greedy tile-stepping movement that slides around buildings; combat with vision-based target acquisition (nearest enemy unit, else nearest enemy building), range + attack cooldown; construction (a worker walks out, lays a barracks, and builds it over BUILD_BARRACKS_TICKS); victory when a side's town hall is razed. A weighted-heuristic MACRO AI (ai_take_turn) can drive EITHER side -- keep workers mining, train up to a worker target, tech to a barracks, pump soldiers, then attack-move the whole army at the enemy hall; auto_step/auto_play_to_end drive BOTH sides for a full self-playing match (validated: a seeded match ends at tick 1788 with a real winner -- barracks built, army fielded, hall razed -- not a stalled cap). checksum() is an FNV-1a fold over the entire state (minerals + every building + every unit + every patch) for cross-process determinism proof. save_data/load_data snapshot the whole match incl. RNG state (NoxDev ABI). GameManager autoload steps the sim in _physics_process for real-time feel; rts_view.gd renders the board in code -- mineral patches, team-coloured buildings + units, HP bars, a live HUD -- with left-drag box selection, right-click context commands (gather/attack/move), and hotkeys to train/build, pause, hand your side to the AI, change sim speed, and restart.

templates.name: Turn-Based 4X (Civilization-lite eXplore/eXpand/eXploit/eXterminate, 2D)
templates.description: A Civilization-lineage TURN-BASED 4X STRATEGY: eXplore a seeded map through fog of war, eXpand by founding cities with settlers, eXploit tiles for food/production/science/gold, and eXterminate rivals by capturing their cities. OUR OWN engine, generic content (no trademarks). TbsEngine is a pure, seedable RefCounted class (no Godot-node/Time dependency) so a whole game replays BYTE-IDENTICALLY from a seed (FNV-1a checksum) and drives headlessly. Real systems: seeded map generation (smoothed random elevation + moisture -> ocean/plains/grass/forest/hill/mountain, edge-biased so continents form inland); per-terrain yields ([food,prod,gold]) + terrain defensive multipliers; two civs (0=player, 1=AI) alternating turns with per-civ FOG OF WAR (seen[] grids revealed by units/cities); CITIES that work their pop-best surrounding tiles each turn -> food drives GROWTH (accumulate to a pop-scaled cost), production drives a BUILD QUEUE, science accumulates civ-wide; a 4-step TECH TREE (bronze_working->spearman, pottery->granary +food, writing->library +science, mathematics->walls +city HP/def) researched in order; UNITS (settler founds cities with min-spacing, warrior, tech-gated spearman) with movement points + greedy 8-dir stepping; HP-BASED COMBAT that is fully DETERMINISTIC (no RNG) using effective-strength ratios with terrain defense, plus a melee advance onto a cleared tile; CITY SIEGE + CAPTURE (garrison defends, else innate city defense trades HP; at 0 HP an adjacent melee unit flips ownership, halves pop, and garrisons); DOMINATION VICTORY (a civ with no cities and no settlers is eliminated) with a score fallback at the turn cap. A weighted-heuristic MACRO AI (ai_take_turn) drives EITHER civ -- research the next tech, build settlers up to a city target then military, expand settlers to the nearest well-spaced spot, and hunt+attack the nearest enemy unit/city -- and auto_step/auto_play_to_end drive BOTH civs for a full self-playing game (validated: a seeded AI-vs-AI game ends at turn 60 with a real winner after expanding to 7 cities and researching all 4 techs, not a cap stall). save_data/load_data snapshot the whole game incl. RNG + fog (NoxDev ABI). GameManager autoload runs the player's turn then hands the AI its turn; tbs_view.gd renders the fogged map, team-coloured cities (with pop + HP) and units in code, with click-select/move/attack, Enter to end turn, F to found, 1-6 to set a city's build, Tab to cycle units, and an auto-play toggle.

templates.name: Tactics SRPG (Fire Emblem/XCOM grid tactics with weapon triangle, 2D)
templates.description: A Fire-Emblem / XCOM-lineage TACTICS SRPG: two armies fight on a grid in alternating TEAM PHASES -- each unit MOVES (Dijkstra move-range over per-terrain cost) then ACTS once (attack / heal / wait). OUR OWN engine, generic content (no trademarks). SrpgEngine is a pure, seedable RefCounted class (no Godot-node/Time dependency) whose single private RNG seeds the map AND every hit/crit roll, so a whole battle -- misses and crits included -- replays BYTE-IDENTICALLY from a seed (FNV-1a checksum) and drives headlessly. It is a REAL SRPG combat kernel, not a sketch: 5 classes (soldier/sword, fighter/axe, knight/lance, archer/bow rng-2, healer/staff) with distinct hp/atk/def/spd/move/range/hit/crit; a WEAPON TRIANGLE (sword>axe>lance>sword; bow+staff neutral) shifting both hit% and damage; hit% from base accuracy + speed + terrain AVOID + triangle; CRITS (3x) from a speed-scaled chance; COUNTERATTACKS when the defender survives and the attacker is in its range; DOUBLE ATTACKS on a >=4 speed lead; terrain that adds avoid + defense + fort HEALING; and dedicated HEALERS that mend the most-wounded ally in range. Movement is a true Dijkstra reachability over terrain move-cost, blocked by enemies, ending only on empty tiles. Win = wipe out the enemy's combat units (a lone healer can't win); a count fallback resolves the turn cap. A weighted-heuristic macro AI (ai_take_phase) drives EITHER team -- for each unit it finds the best (stand-tile, target) pair (favouring kills, damage, accuracy, and hitting healers), advances when nothing is in reach, and lets healers patch the wounded -- and auto_step/auto_play_to_end drive BOTH teams for a full self-playing battle (validated: a seeded 5-v-5 battle ends round 20 with a real winner, 10 units down to 1). save_data/load_data snapshot the whole battle incl. RNG state (NoxDev ABI). GameManager runs the player phase then hands the AI its phase; srpg_view.gd renders terrain + units + HP + the selected unit's move-range and attackable enemies in code, with a classic 2-click flow (select -> move onto a lit tile -> click an in-range enemy to attack, or an ally to heal), plus wait / end-phase / auto / restart.

templates.name: Party CRPG (Baldur's-Gate-lite D&D-5e-lite adventure + initiative combat, 2D)
templates.description: A Baldur's-Gate / Gold-Box-lineage PARTY CRPG on a D&D-5e-LITE ruleset: a 4-hero party (fighter/wizard/cleric/rogue) runs an ADVENTURE PATH of encounters -- initiative-order combats, skill-check EVENTS, and rests -- leveling up until it beats the boss or is wiped. OUR OWN engine, generic content (no trademarks). CrpgEngine is a pure, seedable RefCounted class (no Godot-node/Time dependency) whose single private RNG seeds the party rolls, the encounter path, AND every d20 attack/save/damage roll, so a whole adventure -- crits, misses and all -- replays BYTE-IDENTICALLY from a seed (FNV-1a checksum) and drives headlessly. It is a REAL d20 kernel, not a sketch: six ABILITY SCORES per hero with 5e modifiers + proficiency; class profiles (hit dice, armor, casting stat, extra attacks) -> HP = HD + CON + level, AC = 10 + DEX + armor; INITIATIVE (d20 + DEX) turn order; d20 ATTACK ROLLS vs AC with NAT-20 crits (double damage dice), STR/DEX damage mods, rogue SNEAK ATTACK, and fighter EXTRA ATTACK at level 5; SPELLS off limited slots -- wizard magic-missile (auto-hit) + fireball (an AoE DEX save for half), cleric cure-wounds + bless (party +1 to-hit) -- with a SAVE DC of 8+prof+casting-mod; SAVING THROWS; XP + LEVEL-UP (more HP, more slots, extra attack) awarded on victory. The ADVENTURE PATH is a seeded mix of combat / skill-check event / rest nodes ending in a boss dragon: EVENTS are real d20 ability checks (STR/DEX/INT/WIS/CHA vs a DC) resolved by the best-suited hero, branching to a reward (gold/heal/xp/slot) or a penalty (trap/curse/ambush -- an ambush even injects a surprise fight); RESTS restore HP + slots. Win = clear the boss; lose = a TPK. A weighted-heuristic party+enemy AI drives combat (clerics heal the badly hurt then bless, wizards fireball a crowd else magic-missile, martials focus the lowest-HP foe; enemies focus the weakest hero) and auto_step/auto_play_to_end run the WHOLE adventure headlessly -- balanced to a real challenge (~30%% auto-win, reliably reaching the boss; validated: seed 20260720 clears all 9 nodes to VICTORY at party level 4). save_data/load_data snapshot the whole run incl. RNG state (NoxDev ABI). GameManager runs the party's combat turns while the engine AI resolves enemy turns; crpg_view.gd renders party + enemy stat blocks (HP, level, slots, the active initiative actor), the combat log and encounter progress in code, with click-an-enemy-to-attack (wizard auto-picks fireball vs a crowd) plus H heal / F fireball / B bless / D defend / SPACE resolve-event / A full-auto / R restart.

templates.name: Fighting Game (Street Fighter/MK-lite 1v1 with frame data, 2D)
templates.description: A Street-Fighter / Mortal-Kombat-lineage 1-v-1 FIGHTING GAME run as a DETERMINISTIC FIXED-TIMESTEP sim at 60 ticks/sec with REAL FRAME DATA -- so play is about frame advantage, spacing, blocking high/low, and combos, not ad-hoc timers. OUR OWN engine, generic content (no trademarks). FightEngine is a pure, seedable RefCounted class (no Godot-node/Time dependency) whose single RNG seeds the AI personalities + a little starting jitter and is otherwise pure, so a whole best-of-3 replays BYTE-IDENTICALLY from a seed (FNV-1a checksum) and drives headlessly -- exactly what a rollback/lockstep netcode fighter needs. Real systems: 5 moves (light/heavy punch, light/heavy kick, a special PROJECTILE) each with genuine STARTUP / ACTIVE / RECOVERY frames, damage, hit/blockstun, range and a hit HEIGHT (high overhead / mid / low); a fighter state machine (idle/walk/jump-with-gravity/crouch/block/attack/hitstun/blockstun) driven by a per-tick input {dir, up, down, atk}; BLOCKING by holding away, with correct guard required per height (stand-block overheads, crouch-block lows, chip damage on block); COMBOS via hitstun + special-CANCELS out of cancelable normals that have connected; PUSHBACK + body separation; PROJECTILES that travel and hit; ROUNDS as a best-of-3 with a 60-second timer (higher HP wins a timeout) and KO detection. A heuristic AI (ai_input) with a seeded personality (aggression + reaction) blocks on reaction, anti-airs jump-ins with heavy punch, pokes/combos in range, zones with the projectile, and manages spacing; auto_step/auto_play_to_end drive BOTH fighters for a full self-playing match (validated: a seeded match ends in ~1466 frames with a clean 2-0). save_data/load_data snapshot the whole match incl. RNG state (NoxDev ABI). GameManager steps the sim in _physics_process (60Hz) with the human's sampled inputs; fight_view.gd renders the stage, both fighters with the ACTIVE-frame hitbox extended (so spacing + whiffs read), health bars, round pips, the timer and projectiles -- P1 on A/D move (hold back = block) / W jump / S crouch / F-G-V-B-H attacks, with an attract-mode toggle + restart.

templates.name: Twin-Stick Shooter (Enter the Gungeon/Nuclear Throne-lite arena survival, 2D)
templates.description: An Enter-the-Gungeon / Nuclear-Throne-lineage TWIN-STICK SHOOTER run as a DETERMINISTIC FIXED-TIMESTEP sim at 60 ticks/sec: MOVE with one stick and AIM/FIRE with the other while waves of enemies pour in from the edges. OUR OWN engine, generic content (no trademarks). ShooterEngine is a pure, seedable RefCounted class (no Godot-node/Time dependency) whose single RNG seeds the spawns + enemy jitter and is otherwise pure, so a whole run replays BYTE-IDENTICALLY from a seed (FNV-1a checksum, position-quantized) and drives headlessly. Real systems: independent MOVE + AIM vectors (true twin-stick) with a fire cooldown; player + enemy BULLETS with velocity + lifetime + circle collision; three enemy archetypes with distinct behaviour -- a CHASER that rushes for contact damage, a ranged SHOOTER that keeps mid-range and fires aimed bullets, and a tanky BRUTE -- each with hp/speed/radius/contact/xp; ESCALATING WAVES (count + roster deepen; shooters from wave 2, brutes from wave 3, a brute-heavy final wave) spawned from the arena edges; player I-FRAMES after a hit; SCORE per kill; and a survive-all-waves WIN vs a death LOSE. templates.name: Rhythm Game (Guitar Hero/DDR/osu!mania-lite 4-lane note highway, 2D)
templates.description: A Guitar-Hero / DDR / osu!-mania-lineage RHYTHM GAME run as a DETERMINISTIC FIXED-TIMESTEP sim at 60 ticks/sec: a seeded NOTE CHART scrolls down four lanes toward a hit line and the player taps each lane in time. OUR OWN engine, generic content (no trademarks). RhythmEngine is a pure, seedable RefCounted class (no Godot-node/Time dependency); the RNG only BUILDS the chart and play is otherwise a pure function of the input stream, so a whole song replays BYTE-IDENTICALLY from a seed (FNV-1a checksum) and drives headlessly. Real systems: a seeded procedural CHART generator (BPM-derived beat spacing with syncopation + occasional CHORDS + a no-repeat-lane bias); JUDGMENT BY TIMING WINDOW -- a tap judges against the nearest un-hit note in that lane, Perfect within +/-2 ticks, Good within +/-5, and a note that scrolls past the window unhit becomes a Miss; COMBO + a tiered MULTIPLIER (x1..x4) driving score; ACCURACY (%) and a letter GRADE (S/A/B/C/D); and a proper song end with a final grade. A deterministic AUTO-PLAY seat taps every note exactly on time (full-combo of Perfects, grade S), and a parameterized 'late<N>' policy taps N ticks late so the Good/Miss tiers are demonstrably exercised -- auto_step/auto_play_to_end run a whole song (validated: the perfect seat full-combos an 80-note chart to a grade-S 27500, while a 4-tick-late seat scores strictly worse at 13750, proving the windows are enforced). save_data/load_data snapshot the whole song incl. RNG state (NoxDev ABI). GameManager steps the sim in _physics_process (60Hz) with EDGE-triggered taps; rhythm_view.gd renders the 4-lane highway (notes scrolling to the hit line), lane targets that flash on press, a judgment flash, a progress bar and a score/combo/multiplier/accuracy/grade HUD -- tap D/F/J/K or the arrow keys, with an autoplay toggle + restart.

templates.name: Block Puzzle (Tetris-lite falling-block line-clearer, 2D)
templates.description: A Tetris-lineage FALLING-BLOCK PUZZLE: stack the 7 tetrominoes in a 10x20 well and clear full lines before the stack tops out. OUR OWN engine, generic content (no trademarks). BlockEngine is a pure, seedable RefCounted class (no Godot-node/Time dependency) whose RNG only drives the piece sequence, so a whole game replays BYTE-IDENTICALLY from a seed (FNV-1a checksum) and drives headlessly. Real systems: a seeded 7-BAG randomizer (Fisher-Yates over the 7 types → each appears once per bag, the modern standard); all 7 tetrominoes with their rotation states + real COLLISION; ROTATION with WALL KICKS (tries x offsets 0,-1,+1,-2,+2); gravity on a level-scaled interval plus SOFT DROP + HARD DROP (with drop-distance points); LINE CLEARS that shift the stack down with the classic single/double/triple/TETRIS scoring (100/300/500/800 x level); LEVELS that speed gravity every 10 lines; a NEXT-piece preview; and TOP-OUT game over. It also ships a strong PLACEMENT AI: ai_place enumerates every rotation x column, drops the piece, and scores the resulting board with Dellacherie-style weights (aggregate height, holes, bumpiness, lines cleared), then executes the best placement. auto_step/auto_play_to_end drive a full game (validated: the AI clears 236 lines to level 24 for score 339790 without ever topping out over a 600-piece run). save_data/load_data snapshot the whole game incl. RNG + bag state (NoxDev ABI). templates.name: Idle Clicker (Cookie Clicker-lite incremental with generators + prestige, 2D)
templates.description: A Cookie-Clicker-lineage INCREMENTAL / IDLE game run as a DETERMINISTIC FIXED-TIMESTEP sim at 60 ticks/sec: tap to earn currency, spend it on GENERATORS (each with a x1.15 escalating cost + passive output) and one-off UPGRADES (click + per-building x2 multipliers), and chase seeded GOLDEN bonuses on the way to a prestige-style ASCENSION milestone. OUR OWN engine, generic content (no trademarks). IdleEngine is a pure, seedable RefCounted class (no Godot-node/Time dependency); the ONLY randomness is the seeded golden-bonus schedule, so a whole run replays BYTE-IDENTICALLY from a seed. Real systems: 7 generators (Clicker..Lab) with compounding x1.15 costs + per-building output; 8 one-off UPGRADES gated by ownership (click-power x2s + per-generator x2s + an all-generator x2); a manual click whose power scales with upgrades; GOLDEN bonuses that spawn on a seeded 15-40s cadence and, if tapped in their 5s window, grant either a LUMP (90s of current output, with an early-game floor) or a 7x FRENZY for 7s (both click + passive); and an ASCENSION goal (total-earned milestone) as the prestige seam. Big numbers are handled in doubles and the FNV-1a checksum folds STRING-FORMATTED floats so it never overflows and stays cross-process byte-identical. templates.name: Hidden Object (seek-and-find with decoys, hints + timer, 2D)
templates.description: A seek-and-find HIDDEN-OBJECT casual game: a cluttered scene of seeded item placements, a FIND LIST to locate by clicking, DECOY items that punish misclicks, a limited HINT system, a per-round TIMER, combo + time-bonus scoring, and escalating rounds. OUR OWN engine, generic content (no trademarks). HiddenEngine is a pure, seedable RefCounted class (no Godot-node/Time dependency); one seeded RNG lays out the objects (rejection-sampled with a minimum separation) AND picks the find list, so a whole game -- the exact scene layout every round -- replays BYTE-IDENTICALLY from a seed (FNV-1a checksum over quantized positions) and drives headlessly. Real systems: per-round scene generation of (5+round) TARGET items among (8+round*2) DECOYS placed with min-spacing over the play area; click HIT-TESTING (nearest object within a click radius) that scores a find, ignores/penalizes a decoy, and penalizes an empty-area misclick (breaking the combo); a COMBO that grows the per-find score; a HINT system (3 per round) that reveals + pulses the next unfound target for 2.5s at a score cost; a per-round countdown TIMER (running out = lose) with a time-BONUS on clearing; a live per-round FIND CHECKLIST (name + found state) for the UI; escalating ROUNDS to a MAX_ROUNDS win. A deterministic solver auto-seat clicks each unfound target's exact position (it can see the scene) and uses one hint per round for coverage; auto_step/auto_play_to_end clear a whole game (validated: the solver finds every item across all 4 rounds to a WIN, score 5720, ZERO misclicks -- proving the placement + hit-testing + find-list + scoring + round progression all work). save_data/load_data snapshot the whole game incl. RNG state (NoxDev ABI). GameManager runs the timer in _physics_process; hidden_view.gd renders the play area, the objects (coloured labelled placeholders for real scene art + item sprites), a find-list checklist, a timer bar, the score/hints/misses HUD, and a pulsing hint highlight -- click objects to find them, H for a hint, with an autoplay toggle + restart.
