templates.name: Arcade Sports (top-down 3v3 arcade soccer with team AI, 2D)
templates.description: A top-down ARCADE SPORTS game (arcade SOCCER, NBA-Jam-style end-to-end scoring) run as a DETERMINISTIC FIXED-TIMESTEP sim at 60 ticks/sec: two teams of 3 chase one ball, contest possession, dribble, PASS and SHOOT at goal. OUR OWN engine, generic content (no trademarks). SportsEngine is a pure, seedable RefCounted class (no Godot-node/Time dependency); one seeded RNG only sets the kickoff jitter + a per-team AGGRESSION, and the sim is otherwise pure, so a whole match replays BYTE-IDENTICALLY from a seed (FNV-1a checksum over quantized positions). Real systems: ball PHYSICS (velocity + friction + wall bounces) with POSSESSION (a free ball within a control radius is grabbed by the nearest player), DRIBBLING (the ball rides just ahead of the carrier toward their goal), TACKLING (an opponent within tackle range of a carried ball steals it, with a settle window to stop ping-pong), PASSING (to the best open teammate ahead) and SHOOTING (a distance-scaled deterministic spread so long shots are wilder + miss the mouth more), GOAL DETECTION on the correct mouths, KICKOFFS to the conceding team, a MATCH TIMER, and a decisive full-time WINNER (draw possible). A full TEAM AI drives both sides: the ball-nearest player chases/carries, the carrier shoots in range / passes only under a tight mark / else dribbles at goal (shooting range widens with the team's seeded aggression, so different seeds play differently), and off-ball players hold a ball-shifted formation. auto_step/auto_play_to_end drive a whole AI-vs-AI match (validated: a seeded match ends 14-13 at full time with a decisive winner). save_data/load_data snapshot the whole match incl. RNG state (NoxDev ABI). GameManager steps the sim in _physics_process (60Hz) -- the human steers team 0's active (ball-nearest) player and the AI plays everyone else; sports_view.gd renders the pitch, goals, both teams (the active + ball-owning players ringed), the ball, and a scoreboard + match clock -- WASD/arrows to move, Space to shoot, X to pass, with an attract (both-AI) toggle + restart.

LifeEngine is a pure, seedable RefCounted class (no Godot-node/Time dependency); one seeded RNG only drives the start jitter + the daily events, so a whole life replays BYTE-IDENTICALLY from a seed (FNV-1a checksum over quantized needs). Real systems: SIX NEEDS that decay at per-need rates (tuned so each drains ~1-2 refills per 240-tick day) and, if energy hits 0, force an exhaustion COLLAPSE-sleep; timed ACTIONS (toilet/eat/shower/sleep/relax/socialize) that restore their need over a duration (sleep also pauses most decay); a JOB with a 9-17 work window, a ~4.5h shift paid WAGE x mood and draining energy/hygiene/bladder/fun, one shift/day; a MOOD = the average of needs that scales pay; RELATIONSHIPS with 3 NPCs where socialize deepens your best friendship; a day/CLOCK cycle (10 ticks/hour) with seeded daily events (a bill, a nice chat, waking up inspired); and an ASPIRATION goal (reach a money + best-friend milestone). templates.name: Merge Puzzle (2048-lineage slide-and-merge; a top mobile puzzle, 2D)
templates.description: A 2048-lineage MERGE PUZZLE (the merge mechanic distilled — one of the top mobile puzzle subgenres): slide the 4x4 board in a direction, equal tiles MERGE into the next tier, a new tile spawns, and you climb toward the 2048 tile. OUR OWN engine, generic content (no trademarks). MergeEngine is a pure, seedable RefCounted class (no Godot-node/Time dependency); one seeded RNG drives the tile spawns, so a whole game replays BYTE-IDENTICALLY from a seed (FNV-1a checksum over the grid). Real systems: the canonical 2048 SLIDE-AND-MERGE (per row/column: compact toward the move direction, merge each equal adjacent pair once, accumulate the merged value into score) in all four directions; a seeded SPAWN (a 2, or a 4 at 10%) at a random empty cell after any board-changing move; WIN detection at the 2048 tile (play-on allowed); GAME-OVER detection when the board is full with no adjacent equal pair; and best-tile + move + score tracking. A deterministic corner-heuristic auto-seat (try down, left, right, up in priority — the classic keep-the-big-tile-in-a-corner strategy) plays a full game; auto_step/auto_play_to_end run to the end (validated: the seat plays 138 moves, merges up to the 128 tile for score 1332, to a real game over). save_data/load_data snapshot the whole game incl. RNG state (NoxDev ABI). GameManager applies moves on swipe/keys; merge_view.gd renders the tile grid (coloured by value), the score/best/moves HUD, and a game-over overlay -- arrow keys / WASD / swipe to slide, with an autoplay toggle + restart. The same slide-and-merge core generalises to a Merge-2/3 ITEM board (Gossip Harbor / Merge Dragons) -- see the doc's How-to-extend.

templates.name: Dot IO (Hole.io / Agar.io grow-by-absorbing arena, 2D)
templates.description: A Hole.io / Agar.io-lineage .IO GROW-ARENA game run as a DETERMINISTIC FIXED-TIMESTEP sim (4 of the top-10 downloaded hypercasual games are .io games): steer your 'hole', SWALLOW objects (and rival holes) smaller than your current size to GROW, and out-mass the AI rivals before the timer ends. OUR OWN engine, generic content (no trademarks). DotIoEngine is a pure, seedable RefCounted class (no Godot-node/Time dependency); one seeded RNG places the objects + drives the rival AI, so a whole match replays BYTE-IDENTICALLY from a seed (FNV-1a checksum over quantized state, so it survives cross-process float math). Real systems: an arena of scattered OBJECTS of varied sizes; a size-gated SWALLOW rule (a hole absorbs any object with size <= its own within its radius) that GROWS the hole (radius = sqrt(size)) and scores the swallowed mass, with objects respawning to keep the arena stocked (Hole.io endless growth, tuned to a believable curve); HOLE-vs-HOLE predation (a hole 1.12x bigger swallows a smaller one on overlap -> the small hole respawns at start size); a rival AI that heads for the nearest swallowable object and FLEES a much bigger hole nearby; a 60-second MATCH TIMER; and a WINNER by mass. A deterministic auto-play drives every hole (all-AI) for a full match (validated: the player grows from size 22 to ~4652 for score ~33k and wins the arena; determinism holds cross-process). save_data/load_data snapshot the whole match incl. RNG state (NoxDev ABI). GameManager steps the sim in _physics_process (60Hz) steering the player toward the mouse; dotio_view.gd renders the arena, objects, the player + rival holes sized by mass, a leaderboard + a timer HUD -- move toward the mouse, with an attract (all-AI) toggle + restart.

templates.name: Endless Runner (Subway Surfers / Temple Run 3-lane dodge, 2D)
templates.description: A Subway-Surfers / Temple-Run-lineage ENDLESS RUNNER run as a DETERMINISTIC FIXED-TIMESTEP sim: you auto-run forward down 3 LANES, SWITCH lanes / JUMP / SLIDE to dodge seeded obstacles, grab coins, and survive as the speed ramps up. OUR OWN engine, generic content (no trademarks). RunnerEngine is a pure, seedable RefCounted class (no Godot-node/Time dependency); one seeded RNG lays out the obstacles + coins ahead, so a whole run replays BYTE-IDENTICALLY from a seed (FNV-1a checksum over quantized state). Real systems: an auto-advancing runner whose SPEED ramps with distance; a rolling procedural TRACK spawned ahead (rows of obstacles that TIGHTEN with distance but NEVER block all 3 lanes, guaranteeing a fair path) with three obstacle kinds -- BLOCK (must not share its lane), HURDLE (must be jumping), DUCK (must be sliding) -- plus coin lines on the free lane; lane-switch + timed JUMP + timed SLIDE actions with posture windows; collision that ends the run (recording what you crashed on) unless you cleared the obstacle correctly; a distance+coins SCORE; and a survive-the-distance-cap win. A deterministic DODGE auto-seat routes to the nearest fully-clear lane, jumps hurdles, slides ducks, and drifts for coins when safe; auto_step/auto_play_to_end run a whole run (validated: the seat runs 2029m collecting 38 coins for score 2409 before the ramping speed finally beats a slide). save_data/load_data snapshot the whole run incl. RNG state (NoxDev ABI). GameManager feeds the player's per-tick intent; runner_view.gd renders the 3 lanes, the player with jump/slide posture, scrolling obstacles (colour-coded by kind) + coins, and a distance/coins/score/speed HUD -- A/D lanes, W/Up/Space jump, S/Down slide, with an autoplay toggle + restart.

templates.name: Bubble Shooter (Puzzle Bobble / Bust-a-Move hex match, 2D)
templates.description: A Puzzle-Bobble / Bust-a-Move-lineage BUBBLE SHOOTER run as a DETERMINISTIC sim: a hex-packed grid of coloured bubbles hangs from the ceiling; you AIM a shooter at the bottom and FIRE the current bubble, which flies, BOUNCES off the side walls, and STICKS where it lands. Landing next to 2+ of its own colour POPS the whole connected same-colour group; any bubbles left dangling (no path to the ceiling) then DROP for bonus. Clear the board to WIN; let the stack reach the bottom line and you LOSE. OUR OWN engine, generic content (no trademarks). BubbleEngine is a pure, seedable RefCounted class (no Godot-node/Time dependency); every shot is resolved by a fixed-step ray-march + a nearest-empty-cell hex snap, so the whole match replays BYTE-IDENTICALLY from a seed (FNV-1a checksum over the quantized board). Real systems: proper HEX PACKING (even/odd offset rows with correct 6-neighbour adjacency), wall-bounce ray-march physics, same-colour flood POP (>=3), a ceiling-connectivity flood that DROPS floaters, a next-colour queue drawn from colours still on the board (progress stays possible), and a whole-field DESCENT every N shots that deals a fresh seeded top row (the pressure that ends matches). A deterministic AIM auto-seat evaluates a fan of candidate angles on the real board (no board copy -- it floods a virtual landing cell), maximises pops, else lands as HIGH as possible; auto_step/auto_play_to_end play a whole match (validated: the seat pops 173 bubbles + drops 56 floaters for score 2850 over 76 shots before the stack overruns the line). A performance pass took a full auto-match from 92s to <2s (front-of-stack scan skip + squared-distance collision + no per-angle board duplication). save_data/load_data snapshot the whole board incl. RNG state (NoxDev ABI). GameManager sets the aim + requests a fire; bubble_view.gd renders the hex board, the shooter with its aim line + current/on-deck bubbles, and a score/popped/dropped/shots HUD -- mouse aim, click/Space fire, with an autoplay toggle + restart.
